title: dalbergaria inaequilatera
intro: Dalbergaria inaequilatera, also known as the Strychnine Tree, is a plant species native to tropical regions, characterized by its unique, inaequilateral leaf arrangement and distinctive seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits associated with Dalbergaria inaequilatera include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural insecticide. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying and powdering the leaves, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or applied topically as a poultice.
